name: Empathy Drill — Responding to Emotion (NURSE)
_note: Skill drill. Same 4 metric keys as standard rubric (keeps self-assessment/DB working) but re-pitched to empathic communication. clinical_reasoning slot is repurposed as the Empathy score — the focus of this drill.

anchors.7-8: Occasional minor errors; meaning always clear

anchors.9-10: Empathic phrases sound natural and native-like
Warm, unhurried delivery that fits the emotional moment
Names the emotion, validates it, and explores it before moving on
anchors.5-6: Some acknowledgement but often jumps to facts or reassurance

checklist.item: NAME — named or labelled the patient's emotion (e.g. 'It sounds like you're scared')
UNDERSTAND — expressed understanding of why they feel that way
RESPECT — acknowledged the patient's effort or strength
SUPPORT — offered partnership/support (e.g. 'We'll work through this together')
EXPLORE — invited the patient to say more about the feeling
did NOT jump straight to facts or premature reassurance
avoided false promises (e.g. 'everything will be fine')
gave the patient space without interrupting

empathy_markers: invited the patient to share more
